title: Transient overvoltage and lightning protection of power connected equipment
abstract: The invention is about an overvoltage protection circuit (1) comprising including at least one energy-absorbing module (2), said energy-absorbing module (2) including: - at least one metal-oxide varistor (MOV) (6), - a gas arrestor (7) connected in series with said at least one MOV (6), said overvoltage protection circuit (1) further including a transient discriminative module (4) connected in parallel with the energy absorbing module (2), said transient discriminative module (4) including an avalanche diode (9), wherein - the said at least one energy-absorbing module (2) includes a resistance (8) in parallel with said gas arrestor (7) to form a potential divider circuit with the MOV (6) which shares the voltage about equally across the MOV (6) and the gas arrestor (7), and - the said transient discriminative module (4) including a series connection of the avalanche diode (9) and a capacitor (10).
claims: Claims Claims 1 . An overvoltage protection circuit (1 ) comprising including at least one energy- absorbing module (2), said energy-absorbing module (2) including: - at least one metal-oxide varistor (MOV) (6), - a gas arrestor (7) connected in series with said at least one MOV (6), said overvoltage protection circuit (1 ) further including a transient discriminative module (4) connected in parallel with the energy absorbing module (2), said transient discriminative module (4) including an avalanche diode (9), wherein - the said at least one energy-absorbing module (2) includes a resistance (8) in parallel with said gas arrestor (7) to form a potential divider circuit with the MOV (6) which shares the voltage about equally across the MOV (6) and the gas arrestor (7), and - the said transient discriminative module (4) including a series connection of the avalanche diode (9) and a capacitor (10). 2. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 1 , wherein said energy-absorbing module (2) and said transient discriminative module (4) are connected across an AC power supply ( N). 3. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 2, wherein said energy-absorbing module (2) and said transient discriminative module (4) are connected across two power lines of an AC power supply or across a power line (L) and the neutral line (N) of an AC power supply. 4. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 1 , wherein the circuit (19 including a back-up module (3) connected in parallel with said energy absorbing module (2), said backup module (3) including at least one further metal-oxide varistor (MOV) (1 1 ). 5. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 1 , wherein said energy absorbing module (2) including at least one further fuse element (13) connected in series with said at least one MOV (6). 6. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 4, wherein said back-up module (3) including at least on further fuse element (12) connected in series with said at least one further MOV (1 1 ). 7. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 1 , wherein said the circuit (1 ) including an isolation module (5) connected in series with said energy absorbing module (2), connected between said energy absorbing module (2) and earth (E). 8. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 7, wherein said isolation module (5) including at least one further switching device (14). 9. A circuit (1 ) according to claim 8, wherein the further switching device (14) comprises a further gas arrester (GDT) (14). 10. A method of protecting electrical equipment from overvoltages caused by transient surges on power lines (L, N) of an AC power supply, including the steps of - connecting at least one metal oxide varistor (MOV) (6) across power lines (L, N) to absorb energy from a transient, - connecting a switching device (7) in series with said at least one MOV (6) to isolate said at least one MOV (6) from the AC power supply in the absence of a transient on the power lines, - connecting a resistance (8) in parallel with said switching device (7) so as to form a potential divider circuit with the MOV (6) which shares the voltage about equally across the MOV (6) and the switching device (7), - connecting an avalanche diode (9) in parallel to the switching device (7) to provide high speed clipping the peak from the switching device (7) action in case of a transient surge peak voltage occuring, - connecting a capacitor (10) in series to the avalanche diode (9) to filter out transient overvoltages in a low frequency region from transient surge peak voltages. 1 1 . A method according to claim 10, wherein the switching device (7) comprises a gas arrester (7). 12. A method according to claim 1 1 1 , including the step of connecting a further MOV (1 1 ) in parallel to the series connection of the MOV (6) and the switching device (7) in parallel with the resistor (8). 13. A method according to claim 12, including the step of connecting a further switching device (14) in series to the switching device (7) between the switching device (7) and earth (E).
